commands.repair_reason: - No automated repair is needed; maintainers need to accept or reject the compatibility-sensitive Discord thread identity change before merge.; Cleared: The diff only changes Discord conversation id resolution and unit tests; it adds no dependency, workflow, secret, permission, or code-execution surface. commands.review_summary:
The branch changes Discord inbound conversation resolution to pass thread ids through, resolve threaded inbound turns by raw thread id with parent fallback, and add Discord conversation identity regression tests.

PR surface: Source +18, Tests +68. Total +86 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence path: current main passes threadId into the generic resolver but the Discord plugin drops it before resolving inbound conversation identity. The PR body also supplies redacted before/after Discord thread output showing the bound Codex session and cwd after the patch.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed; maintainers need to accept or reject the compatibility-sensitive Discord thread identity change before merge.

target.title: fix(discord): route thread bindings to plugin owners
target.body:
## Summary

- Route Discord inbound thread conversations by their raw thread id while preserving the parent channel conversation id for configured binding fallback.
- Pass Discord inbound `threadId` through the channel plugin messaging resolver so plugin-owned bindings can claim thread turns.
- Add focused regression tests proving thread conversations use the raw thread id, parent-channel configured bindings still match via `parentConversationId`, exact thread bindings win over parent fallback, and top-level channels keep the `channel:<id>` identity.

Related: #64199, #87721, #84086, #64322.

## Behavior

Discord `/codex bind --cwd ...` follow-up messages in a thread could fall back to the parent OpenClaw agent/session instead of the bound Codex session. The visible symptom was that asking the bound session who it was still answered as the OpenClaw agent, and cwd stayed on the agent workspace instead of the cwd selected during bind.

The Discord plugin already owns the conversation identity rules for this route. This change keeps top-level channel identities prefixed, lets inbound thread messages resolve to the raw Discord thread id so plugin-owned bindings can match the actual bound Codex session, and still carries the parent channel identity so configured parent-channel ACP bindings can claim thread turns.

## Compatibility

This intentionally changes Discord inbound thread conversation identity for plugin-owned binding lookup from the parent `channel:<id>` identity to the raw Discord thread id. The resolver also preserves `parentConversationId` for thread turns, so configured parent-channel bindings can continue matching existing parent-channel routes while exact thread bindings remain higher priority.

This PR does not close #64199. That broader configured ACP session-key isolation issue remains tracked by #64322 or an equivalent follow-up, because this diff only fixes Discord plugin-owned thread binding lookup for inbound thread turns.

## Verification

- `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/discord/src/channel.conversation.test.ts`
- `git diff --check upstream/main..HEAD`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Discord /codex bind --cwd follow-up routing to the bound Codex session instead of the parent OpenClaw agent session.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw Discord gateway logs for the supplied real Discord threads: before thread 1510164477642014740 and after-fix thread 1510357094094733452. Agent names, account details, and local filesystem paths are redacted in the excerpts below.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: In Discord thread 1510357094094733452, bind the native Codex session to the thread with /codex bind --cwd <redacted-selected-project-cwd>, then send follow-up messages in that same thread: who are you, then run pwd.

Evidence after fix: Copied live output from the Codex app-server session bound to Discord thread 1510357094094733452:
```text
2026-05-30T18:59:55.645Z session_meta: id=019e7a41-d67a-7533-8a09-28b034261ce3 cwd=<redacted-selected-project-cwd> originator=openclaw model_provider=openai
2026-05-30T19:00:14Z user: who are you
2026-05-30T19:00:14Z assistant: I’m Codex, your coding agent in this redacted project workspace.
2026-05-30T19:00:14Z user: run pwd
2026-05-30T19:00:14Z assistant: <redacted-selected-project-cwd>
```
Before-fix comparison from Discord thread 1510164477642014740 shows the follow-up stayed on the parent OpenClaw route instead of Codex:
```text
User: who are you
Assistant: <redacted-parent-agent-name>. Technical project architect.
User: run pwd
Assistant: <redacted-parent-agent-workspace-cwd>
User: I mean the cwd of the codex session
Assistant: No visibility into Codex session internals from this parent agent side — you'd need to ask the Codex session directly.
```

Observed result after fix: The after-fix thread 1510357094094733452 routed follow-up messages to the bound native Codex session. The session identified itself as Codex and returned the selected bind cwd, <redacted-selected-project-cwd>, instead of the parent OpenClaw route/workspace.

What was not tested: No known gaps for the Discord plugin-owned thread-binding lookup fixed here. This proof does not close the broader configured ACP per-thread session-key isolation issue tracked separately by #64322.
